2604.18489.title: Aligning Language Models for Lyric-to-Melody Generation with Rule-Based Musical Constraints
2604.18489.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise in lyric-to-melody generation, but models trained with Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) often produce musically implausible melodies with issues like poor rhythm and unsuitable vocal ranges, a phenomenon we term "constraint violation". To address this, we propose a novel alignment framework that instills musical knowledge without human annotation. We define rule-based musical constraints to automatically generate a preference dataset from an SFT model's outputs. The model is then aligned through a sequential process, first using Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) on paired preference data, followed by Kahneman-Tversky Optimization (KTO) on unpaired negative samples. Experimental results demonstrate that our aligned model substantially reduces rule violations and outperforms strong baselines in both objective and subjective evaluations, generating melodies with substantially improved musicality and coherence. An interactive demo with audio comparisons is available at https://arain233.github.io/AligningMelody-demo.
2604.18489.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在歌詞轉旋律生成方面顯示出潛力，但經過監督微調（SFT）訓練的模型經常產生音樂上不合理的旋律，存在節奏不佳和不適合的音域等問題，我們稱之為「約束違反」。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一種新穎的對齊框架，該框架在不需要人工標註的情況下灌輸音樂知識。我們定義了基於規則的音樂約束，從SFT模型的輸出自動生成偏好數據集。然後，通過一個序列過程對模型進行對齊，首先在配對的偏好數據上使用直接偏好優化（DPO），然後在未配對的負樣本上使用卡尼曼-特維爾斯基優化（KTO）。實驗結果顯示，我們的對齊模型顯著減少了規則違反，並在客觀和主觀評估中超越了強基準，生成了音樂性和連貫性顯著改善的旋律。可在 https://arain233.github.io/AligningMelody-demo 獲得帶有音頻比較的互動演示。
2604.18489.author: Hao Meng et.al.
2604.18489.authors: Hao Meng, Siyuan Zheng, Shuran Zhou, Qiangqiang Wang, Yang Song
